title: Earth Brain Event
description: Integrated schema for Earth Brain OS v0.2 Modular Event Architecture. This schema defines the top-level Earth Brain event envelope and references eight independent layer schemas: AI Agent, Optical Nervous, Knowledge Cortex, Trace Attribution, Royalty Circulation, Kazene Regulation, Defense Immune, and Human Governance.

event_id.description: Stable identifier for this Earth Brain event.

event_version.description: Version of this Earth Brain event schema or implementation profile.

created_at.description: Creation time of this Earth Brain event.

updated_at.description: Optional last update time of this Earth Brain event.

status.description: Operational status of this integrated Earth Brain event.

description.description: Short explanation of this integrated Earth Brain event.

layer_order.description: Recommended logical order of the modular Earth Brain OS layers.

cross_layer_links.description: Explicit links between layers inside this integrated Earth Brain event.

architecture_name.const: Earth Brain OS Modular Event Architecture

architecture_version.description: Architecture version for this modular event model.

schema_profile.description: Schema profile used by this integrated event.

integration_mode.description: How this schema integrates layer schemas.

layer_count.description: Number of required Earth Brain OS layers in v0.2.

design_principles.description: Design principles used by this modular event architecture.

context_id.description: Optional context identifier for this event.

context_type.description: Type of context represented by this event.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the event context.

related_documents.description: Optional references to documents, specifications, repositories, or notes related to this event.

tags.description: Optional tags for search, classification, or documentation.

layers.description: Container for the eight modular Earth Brain OS layer schemas.

link_id.description: Stable identifier for the cross-layer link.

relationship.description: Relationship between the source and target layer.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of this cross-layer link.

event_types.description: Optional event types exchanged through this link.

required.description: Whether this cross-layer link is required for this event.

layer_validation_required.description: Whether each referenced layer must be validated against its own layer schema.

cross_layer_consistency_required.description: Whether cross-layer consistency checks are required.

missing_layer_policy.description: Policy for handling missing required layers.

version_alignment_policy.description: Policy for aligning layer versions with the integrated event version.

failure_policy.description: Policy for handling validation, reference, or integration failure.

future_lifecycle_ref.description: Optional future reference to lifecycle state machine schema planned for v0.3.

trace_required.description: Whether the integrated Earth Brain event must produce trace records.

layer_trace_required.description: Whether each layer must expose its own trace requirements.

cross_layer_trace_required.description: Whether cross-layer links and event flows must be traceable.

provenance_required.description: Whether provenance must be preserved across the integrated event.

minimum_provenance_fields.description: Minimum provenance fields required at the integrated event level.

retention_policy.description: Trace retention policy for the integrated event.

human_review_boundary.description: Boundary condition that triggers integrated human review.

governance_layer_required.description: Whether the Human Governance Layer is required for this integrated event.

policy_refs.description: Policy documents, rules, or schemas applied to the integrated event.

constitutional_refs.description: Foundational principles, charters, constitutions, or governance references.

escalation_channels.description: Channels or layers used for escalation.

failure_modes.description: Known integrated event failure modes to monitor.
